title: SEC 10-K / 10-Q Risk Factor Sentiment Delta
summary: Build a lightweight SEC filing sentiment monitor that downloads Item 1A (Risk Factors) from 10-K/10-Q filings via EDGAR's free API, scores them with FinBERT or Claude, and flags when a company's tone has shifted meaningfully from its prior filing. Research on 21,421 10-K reports (2002-2024) shows that a more optimistic tone in risk factors predicts higher returns, and that stocks with the lowest 'positive language similarity' to their prior filings (i.e. language changed most) generate the strongest alpha — consistent with Lazy Prices research. This is a weekly/quarterly signal, not daily, making it a position bias layer rather than an entry trigger.
expected_impact: ScienceDirect 2025 study (21K reports, 2002-2024): more optimistic risk-factor tone predicts higher returns at 5% significance; 'low positive similarity' decile outperforms 'high positive similarity' — adds slow-burn fundamental signal
